title: Prefer SSE for one-way server-to-client streams
description: Use Server-Sent Events for unidirectional updates and reserve WebSocket for genuine bidirectional needs.
body: Use when choosing a transport for live updates. Do not reach for WebSocket by default when the client only consumes a stream (notifications, progress, dashboards). Actions: choose SSE for server-to-client only since it runs over plain HTTP, auto-reconnects with Last-Event-ID, and traverses proxies easily; choose WebSocket when the client must also send frequent low-latency messages (chat, collaboration, games). Validation signals include verifying SSE reconnect resumes from Last-Event-ID and that the chosen transport survives the deployment's proxies. Risk boundary: forcing bidirectional WebSocket where SSE suffices adds connection and auth-refresh complexity without benefit; note the tradeoff.
